description: This image displays a grayscale mosaic of three contiguous Sentinel-1 Interferometric Wide (IW) mode sub-swaths, labelled IW3 (left), IW2 (middle), and IW1 (right), showing a land and coastal area. Each sub-swath panel is horizontally segmented by nine thin black lines, representing individual radar bursts. The imagery shows varied land cover, including agricultural fields, urban areas, and water bodies. The IW2 sub-swath includes a large inland lake or estuary at the top, while the IW1 sub-swath on the right distinctly depicts a coastline with a large expanse of water to the east, consistent with a coastal region of the UK. The varying gray tones indicate different radar backscatter intensities, reflecting surface roughness and dielectric properties.
